scenarios.situation: Teacher says: 'Can you please pay attention?'
scenarios.whatItMeans: The teacher noticed you're distracted and wants you to focus on the lesson.
suggestedResponses: Sorry, I'll focus now.
Can you repeat that? I want to make sure I understand.
I'm listening now, thank you.

scenarios.whatItMeans: Your friend might actually be upset or hurt, but is trying to hide their feelings.
suggestedResponses: Are you okay? You seem upset.
Did I do something wrong?
I care about what you think. Can we talk about it?

scenarios.whatItMeans: Your parent is not saying yes or no right now. They need time to think about it.
suggestedResponses: When will you decide?
Can I help you decide?
Okay, I'll wait to hear.

scenarios.situation: Classmate says: 'That's so cool!' (with an eye roll)
scenarios.whatItMeans: They're being sarcastic - they actually think it's NOT cool.
suggestedResponses: I can tell you're joking.
You don't seem to really think so.
It's okay if you don't like it.

scenarios.whatItMeans: They're not actually fine. They might need support but don't want to burden you.
suggestedResponses: You don't seem fine. I'm here if you want to talk.
It's okay to not be okay.
Want to hang out? Sometimes that helps.

scenarios.situation: Sibling says: 'Can you just leave me alone?'
scenarios.whatWasSaid: Can you just leave me alone?
scenarios.whatItMeans: Your sibling needs some personal space right now. They might be stressed or need quiet time.
suggestedResponses: Okay, I'll give you some space.
Let me know when you want to talk.
Sure, I understand.

scenarios.situation: Teacher says: 'This is the third time I'm asking.'
scenarios.whatWasSaid: This is the third time I'm asking.
scenarios.whatItMeans: The teacher is getting frustrated because they've repeated themselves. They need you to listen carefully now.
suggestedResponses: I'm sorry, I'll listen more carefully.
Can you write it down so I don't forget?
I understand now, thank you for your patience.

scenarios.whatItMeans: Your friend is noticing a pattern in your behavior that bothers them. 'Always' is an exaggeration but shows they're upset.
suggestedResponses: I didn't realize I was doing that. Can you help me notice it?
I'm sorry, I'll try to work on that.
Can you tell me when I do it so I can stop?
